name: Mortgage Calculator
tagline: Calculate your monthly mortgage payment, total interest, and amortization schedule
keywords: mortgage calculator, home loan calculator, mortgage payment
calcJS: var p=f('homePrice');var dp=f('downPayment')/100;var n=f('loanTerm')*12;var r=f('interestRate')/100/12;var loan=p*(1-dp);var mp=loan*(r*Math.pow(1+r,n))/(Math.pow(1+r,n)-1);var ti=mp*n-loan;return{monthlyPayment:$(mp),totalInterest:$(ti),totalCost:$(mp*n)};

faqs.q: How is the monthly payment calculated?
faqs.a: We use the standard amortization formula: M = P[r(1+r)^n]/[(1+r)^n – 1], where P is the loan amount, r is the monthly interest rate, and n is the number of payments.
Private Mortgage Insurance (PMI) is required when your down payment is less than 20%. It typically costs 0.5-1% of the loan amount per year and can be removed once you reach 20% equity.

faqs.q: Should I choose a 15-year or 30-year mortgage?
faqs.a: A 15-year mortgage has higher monthly payments but saves significantly on total interest. A 30-year mortgage keeps payments lower but costs more overall. Choose based on your monthly budget and long-term goals.

faqs.q: What credit score do I need for a mortgage?
faqs.a: Most conventional loans require a minimum 620 credit score. FHA loans allow scores as low as 580 with 3.5% down. Better scores get lower interest rates, potentially saving tens of thousands over the loan term.

faqs.q: How much house can I afford?
faqs.a: A common rule is the 28/36 rule: spend no more than 28% of gross income on housing costs and no more than 36% on total debt. Include mortgage payment, property tax, insurance, and HOA fees in your housing costs.

chart.title: Mortgage Comparison — 30-Year Fixed
chart.subtitle: Monthly payments for different home prices at 6.5% interest with 20% down

howItWorksIntro: This calculator uses the standard amortization formula to compute your mortgage payment.
howItWorksRules: n = loan term in years × 12 (total payments)
Total Interest = (Monthly Payment × n) – Loan Amount
howItWorksOutro: This estimate does not include property tax, homeowner's insurance, PMI, or HOA fees. Your actual monthly housing cost will be higher.
